info.title: Katisha User Service
info.description:
Handles authentication, user management, organization management, and IAM for the Katisha platform.

## Authentication

All protected endpoints require a valid JWT access token (15 min TTL by default).

**Mobile clients** — include `X-Client-Type: mobile` and send `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.
Tokens are returned in the response body.

**Web clients** — the server sets `access_token` and `refresh_token` as `HttpOnly` cookies automatically.
No `Authorization` header is needed; cookies are sent back with every request.

## IAM — Roles & Permissions

Permissions are stored as **grant patterns** on roles in the database and packed into the JWT
as CASL rules at login time. The `authorize(action, subject)` middleware checks rules at the
route level; object-level scoping is enforced inside the service layer.

Grant pattern format: `{subject}:{action}:{scope}` — e.g. `user:read:org`, `*:*:platform`.

| Scope | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `own` | Caller can only act on their own record |
| `org` | Caller can act on any record within their org |
| `platform` | Unrestricted — all orgs |

Built-in role slugs:

| Role | Effective scope |
|---|---|
| `platform-admin` | `*:*:platform` — full access |
| `org-admin` | `*:*:org` — full access within their org |
| `dispatcher` | `user:invite:org`, `user:read:org`, `audit_log:read:org` |
| `driver` | `user:read:own`, `user:update:own` |
| `passenger` | `user:read:own`, `user:update:own` |

## Organization Application Flow (public, no auth)

New organizations apply through a 3-step self-service flow:

1. **Get presigned URLs** — `GET /organizations/apply/documents/presigned-url` twice
   (once per document type) and upload files directly to SeaweedFS.
2. **Submit application** — `POST /organizations/apply` with document paths.
   Creates the org in `pending` status and sends an OTP to `contact_email`.
3. **Verify contact** — `POST /organizations/verify-contact` with the OTP.
   Sets `contact_email_verified_at` and triggers admin review notification.

Platform admins then use `POST /organizations/:id/approve` or `PATCH /organizations/:id`
(with `status: rejected`) to act on the application.

## Presigned URL Upload Pattern

Avatar, logo, and document uploads never transit the Node.js server:

1. Call the relevant `presigned-url` endpoint → receive `{ upload_url, path }`.
2. `PUT` the file directly to `upload_url` with the **exact same** `Content-Type` header.
3. Commit by sending `path` in the subsequent `PATCH` or `POST` body.

The presigned URL expires in ~5 min (configurable via `S3_PRESIGNED_EXPIRES_IN`).
The full CDN URL is never stored — build it client-side: `CDN_BASE_URL + "/" + path`.

## Push Notifications

The mobile app obtains an FCM token from the Firebase SDK and registers it via
`POST /auth/register-device`. This switches `notif_channel` to `app`. Subsequent
security events (new device login, password change, account suspension) and org lifecycle
events are delivered as push notifications based on the user's `notif_channel` preference.

| `notif_channel` | Delivery |
|---|---|
| `sms` | SMS only |
| `email` | Email only |
| `app` | Push (falls back to SMS if no FCM token) |
| `all` | SMS + email + push |

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
Registration, login, 2FA, password reset, token lifecycle, device registration.
Start here — a valid access token is required by all other sections.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description:
Organization CRUD, two-step approval workflow, logo uploads.
Understand and create organizations before inviting users into them.

tags.name: Organization Applications
tags.description:
Public self-service flow for new organizations to apply for Katisha membership.
No authentication required. Three ordered steps: upload documents → submit application → verify contact email.

tags.name: Users
tags.description:
User profiles, avatar uploads, staff invitations, 2FA toggle, user administration.
Requires an organization to exist before staff can be invited into it.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description:
Role and grant management. Roles are collections of grant patterns (`subject:action:scope`).
Managed roles (`is_managed: true`) are seeded by the platform and cannot be mutated via API.
`platform-admin` can manage all non-managed roles; `org-admin` can only manage roles within their own org.

tags.name: Permissions
tags.description:
Read-only catalog of all valid permission subjects and actions.
Use `GET /permissions` to build the pattern picker when creating or editing roles.

BearerAuth.description: Mobile clients only — send `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` with `X-Client-Type: mobile`.

CookieAuth.name: access_token
CookieAuth.description: Web clients only — set automatically by the server.

ClientType.name: X-Client-Type
ClientType.description:
Omit for web clients (tokens returned as `HttpOnly` cookies).
Send `mobile` to receive tokens in the JSON response body instead.

AuthUser.description: Minimal user object returned alongside tokens on every successful auth response.

Tokens.description:
Present in the JSON body **only** for mobile clients (`X-Client-Type: mobile`).
For web clients tokens are delivered as `HttpOnly` cookies and omitted from the body.

access_token.description: Short-lived JWT (15 min by default). Send as `Authorization: Bearer` on subsequent requests.

refresh_token.description: Long-lived opaque token. Use with `POST /auth/refresh` to rotate.

CaslRule.description:
A single CASL rule packed into the JWT at login. The frontend can use these to gate
UI elements without extra API calls. Never rely on client-side rule evaluation for
security decisions — the server re-enforces them on every request.

UserMePassenger.description: Profile returned by `GET /users/me` and `PATCH /users/me` for passenger accounts.

email.description: Always null for passengers — email is a staff-only field.

notif_channel.description:
Controls which channel(s) are used for security and lifecycle notifications.
Defaults to `sms` for passengers. Automatically switches to `app` when an FCM
token is registered via `POST /auth/register-device`.

UserMeStaff.description: Profile returned by `GET /users/me` and `PATCH /users/me` for staff accounts. Includes roles and packed CASL rules.

permissions.description: Packed CASL rules derived from this user's DB role-permission assignments. Use for frontend feature-flag rendering.

notif_channel.description:
Controls notification delivery. Defaults to `all` for staff (SMS + email + push).
Automatically switches to `app` when an FCM token is registered.

phone_number.description: Masked to `+250***123` format for non-admin callers. Full number for platform admins.

last_login_at.description: Included only for platform admin callers.

UserFullProfile.description: Full user profile returned by `GET /users/:id` and `PATCH /users/:id`.

driver_license_number.description: Admin only — omitted for non-admin callers.

driver_license_verified_at.description: Admin only — omitted for non-admin callers.

last_login_at.description: Admin only — omitted for non-admin callers.

OrgCreated.description: Response body for `POST /organizations`. Lighter shape — logo and approval fields are not set yet.

OrgFull.description:
Full organization detail. Returned by `GET /organizations/:id`, `GET /organizations/me`,
`PATCH /organizations/:id`, and `POST /organizations/:id/approve`.
Some fields are omitted for non-admin callers (see field descriptions).

child_orgs.description: Admin only — omitted for non-admin callers.

approved_by.description: Admin only — UUID of the platform-admin who ran the final approval.

Grant.description: A single grant pattern assigned to a role.

is_managed.description: Managed grants are seeded by the platform and cannot be deleted via API.

Role.description: A named collection of grant patterns.

is_managed.description: Managed roles cannot be renamed or deleted via API.

Permission.description: A single entry from the permission catalog — valid (subject, action) pairs recognized by the platform.

code.description: Canonical identifier. Use as the `subject:action` part of a grant pattern.

post.description:
Creates a `passenger` account in `pending_verification` status.
On success, sends **separate** 6-digit OTPs per channel:
- **Phone OTP** → always sent via SMS (`phone_verification` purpose)
- **Email OTP** → only if `email` provided, distinct code via email (`email_verification` purpose)

The account cannot log in until at least one channel is verified.
The first channel verified sets `login_channel` and activates the account.
After verifying, call `POST /auth/login` to obtain tokens.

Staff accounts are provisioned via `POST /users/invite` (admin-only flow).

email.description: Optional. If provided, the OTP is also delivered here and `notif_channel` defaults to `[sms, email]`.

locale.description: Language preference for notifications. Defaults to `rw` (Kinyarwanda).

201.description: Account created — OTP sent to phone (and email if provided)

409.description: Phone number or email already registered

post.description:
Verifies the phone OTP. On first verification (account `pending_verification`):
activates the account and sets `login_channel` to `phone`.

**Does not issue tokens.** Returns `login_identifier` so the client can direct
the user to the login screen with the correct identifier pre-filled.

If the account was already active (email verified first), only stamps `phone_verified_at`.
The user can then switch to phone login via `POST /users/me/login-channel`.

message.example: Phone verified. Please log in with your phone number.

post.description:
Verifies the email OTP. On first verification (account `pending_verification`):
activates the account and sets `login_channel` to `email`.

**Does not issue tokens.** Returns `login_identifier` so the client can direct
the user to the login screen.

If the account was already active (phone verified first), only stamps `email_verified_at`.
The user can then switch to email login via `POST /users/me/login-channel`.

message.example: Email verified. Please log in with your email.

post.description:
Called after `POST /auth/login` returns `{ requires_verification: true }`.

Verifies the OTP sent to the channel the user logged in with, activates the account,
sets `login_channel`, and issues the first token pair. This is the **only** verification
endpoint that returns tokens.

channel.description: Must match the channel returned by POST /auth/login.

get.description:
Called by the frontend when the accept-invite page loads (before showing the set-password form).
Returns masked contact details so the user knows who the invite is for.
Returns `404` if the token is invalid or already used, `410` if expired.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Raw invite token from the URL

404.description: Token not found or already used

post.summary: Login with phone or email + password
post.description:
Accepts a phone number in E.164 format or an email address as `identifier`.

**Normal** → `200` with full `AuthResponse`.

**Account pending verification** → `202` with `{ requires_verification: true, user_id, channel, expires_in }`.
An OTP is sent to the channel the user logged in with. Follow up with `POST /auth/verify-login`.

**2FA enabled** → `202` with `{ requires_2fa: true, user_id, expires_in }`.
An OTP is sent to the user's phone. Follow up with `POST /auth/verify-2fa`.

**Wrong login channel** → `403` with `PHONE_LOGIN_REQUIRED` or `EMAIL_LOGIN_REQUIRED`
if the user tries to log in via a channel that is not their `login_channel`.

Rate-limited to **5 attempts per 15 min per IP** by default.
Configurable via `RATE_LIMIT_LOGIN_MAX` and `RATE_LIMIT_LOGIN_WINDOW_SECONDS`.

403.description: Account suspended (`ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED`) or phone not yet verified (`PHONE_NOT_VERIFIED`)

post.description:
Step 2 when `POST /auth/login` returns `requires_2fa: true`.
Verifies the OTP sent to the user's phone and issues tokens. The OTP is single-use.

Sends a 6-digit OTP to the user's phone or email depending on whether `identifier` is
a phone number or email address.

Always returns `204` regardless of whether the identifier exists — this prevents
user enumeration attacks.

Rate-limited to **3 attempts per hour per IP** by default.
Configurable via `RATE_LIMIT_RESET_MAX` and `RATE_LIMIT_RESET_WINDOW_SECONDS`.

204.description: OTP sent (or silently ignored if identifier not found)

post.description:
Verifies the OTP issued by `POST /auth/forgot-password` and sets a new password.
All existing refresh token sessions are revoked immediately.
A `security.password_changed` notification is sent via SMS, email, and push.

Issues a new access + refresh token pair and invalidates the old refresh token.

**Mobile** — send `Authorization: Bearer <refresh_token>` with `X-Client-Type: mobile`.

**Web** — the refresh token is read from the `refresh_token` cookie automatically.

**Reuse detection:** if an already-revoked token is submitted, all sessions for that
user are wiped and a `security.all_sessions_revoked` notification is sent via SMS and push.

401.description: Refresh token missing, invalid, expired, or user is suspended

post.description:
Revokes the current session's refresh token. Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.

**Mobile** — send refresh token via `Authorization: Bearer` with `X-Client-Type: mobile`.
**Web** — reads from the `refresh_token` cookie; clears both auth cookies on response.

Revokes all refresh tokens for the authenticated user across all devices.
Requires a valid access token (not a refresh token).

Issues a fresh 6-digit OTP to a user still in `pending_verification` status.

- `channel: 'phone'` (default) → resends `phone_verification` OTP via SMS.
- `channel: 'email'` → resends `email_verification` OTP via email (requires email on account, not yet verified).
- The previous OTP for the same channel+purpose is invalidated automatically.
- Rate-limited to 3 requests per 10 minutes per `user_id`.

channel.description: Which channel to resend the OTP to.

post.description:
Stores the Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) token for the authenticated user's device
and switches `notif_channel` to `app`.

After this call, security events (new device login, password change, account suspension)
and org lifecycle events are delivered as push notifications to this device.
If `notif_channel` was previously `all`, push is added on top of SMS and email.

Call once after the mobile app obtains a token from the Firebase SDK.
Re-call whenever Firebase refreshes the token (e.g. after app reinstall).

fcm_token.description: FCM registration token from the Firebase SDK.

204.description: FCM token saved — notif_channel switched to app

post.description:
Creates an organization directly. Use this for orgs provisioned by Katisha staff
on behalf of a client. For the self-service public flow, see `POST /organizations/apply`.

**Required permission:** `create:Org`
→ `platform-admin`

The org name is slugified automatically. Duplicate names return `409`.

parent_org_id.description: Parent cooperative org for member companies.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:Org`

Platform admins see all orgs. `org-admin` and `dispatcher` see only their own org.

Returns the organization the authenticated user belongs to (`org_id` from their JWT).
No permission gate — any authenticated user with an `org_id` may call this.
Returns `404` for users with no org (passengers, platform admins).

**Required permission:** `read:Org`

Non-admin callers (`org-admin`, `dispatcher`) may only read their own org.
The service returns `403` if the caller's `org_id` does not match the requested `id`.

patch.description:
**Required permission:** `update:Org`

Field-level rules:
- `status` and `rejection_reason` — platform admins only. `org-admin` callers receive `403` if these fields are included.
- `logo_path` — use `path` from the logo presigned-url endpoint. Pass `null` to remove (S3 file deleted asynchronously).
- `status: suspended` — blacklists all active access tokens for this org's users in Redis (15 min TTL). Sends `org.suspended` notifications directly to the org contact and fans out to all active org users via their `notif_channel` preference.
- `status: rejected` — sends `org.rejected` notifications to the org contact.
- `status: active` — can be used by admins to directly approve without the two-step flow.

logo_path.description: S3 path from logo presigned-url. null removes the logo.

status.description: Admin only. suspended triggers token blacklist and notifications.

rejection_reason.description: Admin only. Include when setting status to rejected.

post.description:
Handles a **two-step approval** process. Behavior depends on the caller's role:

---

**Step 1 — Cooperative pre-approval** (`org-admin` of the parent cooperative)

Applies only to `cooperative` child orgs that require parent sign-off.
Sets `cooperative_approved_at` on the child org. Org status remains `pending`.
Sends an `org.cooperative_approved` SMS to the child org's contact phone.
The caller's `org_id` must match the child org's `parent_org_id`, otherwise `403`.

---

**Step 2 — Final Katisha approval** (`platform-admin`)

Transitions the org from `pending` to `active`. Sets `approved_by` and `approved_at`.

For `cooperative` orgs, Step 1 must be completed first — attempting Step 2 without
`cooperative_approved_at` set returns `400 COOPERATIVE_APPROVAL_REQUIRED`.

On final approval:
- Creates an `Invitation` record for the org contact to register as `org-admin`.
- Sends `org_approved.sms` and `org_approved.mail` to the org contact with the invite link (7-day expiry).

---

**Required permission:** `update:Org`

200.description:
Step 1: Pre-approved (status still `pending`, `cooperative_approved_at` is set).
Step 2: Fully approved (status `active`, invite sent to org contact).

400.description:
Org is not in `pending` status (`ORG_NOT_PENDING`) or
cooperative pre-approval has not been completed (`COOPERATIVE_APPROVAL_REQUIRED`).

get.summary: Get presigned PUT URL for own org's logo
get.description:
Returns a short-lived S3 presigned URL for uploading the organization logo directly to SeaweedFS.
Intended for `org-admin` users to manage their own org's logo.

**Required permission:** `update:Org`

**Upload flow:**
1. Call with `?content_type=image/png`.
2. `PUT` the image to `upload_url` with the matching `Content-Type` header.
3. `PATCH /organizations/:id` with `{ "logo_path": "<path>" }` to persist.

To **remove** the logo: `PATCH /organizations/:id` with `{ "logo_path": null }`.
Build the CDN URL client-side: `CDN_BASE_URL + "/" + logo_path`.

upload_url.description: PUT the image here. Expires in ~5 min.

400.description:
Missing `content_type` query parameter (`MISSING_CONTENT_TYPE`), or the authenticated
user has no `org_id` (`NO_ORG` — should not occur for a valid `org-admin`).

get.summary: Get presigned PUT URL for any org's logo (admin)
get.description:
Same upload flow as `GET /organizations/me/logo/presigned-url` but targets a specific
org by ID. Intended for platform admins to manage logos for any org.

**Required permission:** `update:Org`
→ `platform-admin` in practice

upload_url.description: PUT the image here. Expires in ~5 min.

get.summary: Step 1 — Get presigned PUT URL for an application document
get.description:
Returns a short-lived S3 presigned URL for uploading org application documents
directly to SeaweedFS. **No authentication required.**

Call this endpoint **twice** before submitting an application — once per document type.
Collect both returned `path` values and pass them to `POST /organizations/apply`.

Accepted content types per document type:

| `doc_type` | Accepted `content_type` |
|---|---|
| `business_certificate` | `application/pdf` only |
| `rep_id` | `application/pdf`, `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/webp` |

The presigned URL expires in ~5 min. Upload must complete before expiry.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: MIME type of the file. Must exactly match the Content-Type header used in the PUT request.

upload_url.description: PUT the document here with the matching Content-Type header. Expires in ~5 min.

path.description: Pass as business_certificate_path or rep_id_path in POST /organizations/apply.

415.description: Content type not allowed for the given document type

post.description:
Public endpoint — no authentication required.

Creates an org record in `pending` status with the uploaded document paths and sends
a 6-digit OTP to `contact_email` for ownership verification.

**Prerequisites:** upload both documents using `GET /organizations/apply/documents/presigned-url`
and supply the returned `path` values as `business_certificate_path` and `rep_id_path`.

No admin review is triggered until `POST /organizations/verify-contact` succeeds.
The OTP expires after **10 minutes**. If it expires, the application must be resubmitted
(duplicate name check will return `409` — applicant should contact support).

contact_email.description: A 6-digit OTP will be emailed here for verification.

parent_org_id.description: ID of the parent cooperative org

business_certificate_path.description: S3 path from GET /organizations/apply/documents/presigned-url with doc_type=business_certificate.

rep_id_path.description: S3 path from GET /organizations/apply/documents/presigned-url with doc_type=rep_id.

202.description: Application received — OTP sent to contact_email

message.example: Application received. Please check your email for a verification code.

409.description: Organization name or slug already registered

post.summary: Step 3 — Verify org contact email with OTP
post.description:
Public endpoint — no authentication required.

Verifies the 6-digit OTP emailed during `POST /organizations/apply`.

On success:
- Sets `contact_email_verified_at` on the org record.
- Sends a confirmation SMS + email to the applicant.
- Notifies all active platform admins (users with `platform-admin` role) via email to trigger manual review.

Katisha admins then use `POST /organizations/:id/approve` or
`PATCH /organizations/:id` with `status: rejected` to finalize the application.

409.description: Contact email already verified for this org

get.description:
Returns the authenticated user's profile. Response shape differs by `user_type`:
- `passenger` → `UserMePassenger`
- `staff` → `UserMeStaff` (includes role slugs and packed CASL permission rules)

patch.description:
Updates the authenticated user's own profile. At least one field must be provided.

Field notes:
- `email` — rejected for passenger accounts (`PASSENGERS_CANNOT_HAVE_EMAIL` 422). Staff only.
- `avatar_path` — use the `path` from `GET /users/me/avatar/presigned-url` after uploading. Pass `null` to remove the current avatar (S3 file is deleted asynchronously).
- `notif_channel` — controls how notifications are delivered. Setting `app` without a registered FCM token will leave push delivery non-functional until `POST /auth/register-device` is called.

email.description: Staff only. Rejected with 422 for passengers.

avatar_path.description: S3 path from the presigned-url endpoint. null removes the avatar.

get.summary: Get presigned PUT URL for own avatar
get.description:
Returns a short-lived S3 presigned URL for uploading an avatar image directly
to SeaweedFS. The user-service never handles the file bytes.

**Upload flow:**
1. Call with `?content_type=image/jpeg` (or `png`/`webp`/`gif`).
2. `PUT` the image to `upload_url` with the **exact same** `Content-Type` header.
3. `PATCH /users/me` with `{ "avatar_path": "<path>" }` to persist.

To **remove** the avatar: `PATCH /users/me` with `{ "avatar_path": null }`.
Build the full CDN URL client-side: `CDN_BASE_URL + "/" + path`.

upload_url.description: PUT the image file here. Expires in ~5 min.

path.description: Use this as avatar_path in PATCH /users/me.

post.description:
Returns `204` if the supplied password matches the user's current hash.
Use as a confirmation gate before sensitive operations (e.g. before displaying secrets
or allowing 2FA changes).

post.summary: Request login channel change (step 1 — sends OTP)
post.description:
Sends a fresh OTP to the requested channel to confirm possession before switching `login_channel`.
The target channel must be associated with the account (phone_number always exists; email must be present).
Requires authentication.

409.description: Already active channel or email already verified conflict

post.summary: Confirm login channel change (step 2 — verifies OTP, updates channel)
post.description:
Verifies the OTP sent by `POST /users/me/login-channel` and updates `login_channel`.
Also stamps the corresponding `_verified_at` field.
Requires authentication.

patch.description:
Toggles two-factor authentication for the authenticated user.
On change, a notification is sent via the user's `notif_channel`
(`security.2fa_enabled` or `security.2fa_disabled`).

post.description:
Creates an `Invitation` record (7-day expiry) and sends an invite link via SMS and/or email.
At least one of `phone_number` or `email` must be provided.

The invitee calls `POST /users/accept-invite` with the raw token to activate their account.

**Required permission:** `invite:User`
→ `platform-admin`, `org-admin`, `dispatcher`

`org-admin` callers are automatically scoped to their own org — the `org_id` body field
is ignored and the caller's `org_id` is used instead.

role_slug.description: Must match an existing role slug.

org_id.description: Target org. Ignored for org-admin callers.

locale.description: Language preference for the invitee. Stored on the invitation and applied to the user account when the invite is accepted. Defaults to `rw`.

invite_token.description: Raw invite token. Embed in the invite link as `?token=<value>` and send to the invitee.

post.summary: Accept a staff invitation (set password, trigger OTP)
post.description:
Sets the user's password, creates the account in `pending_verification` status,
and sends OTP(s) to the provided channel(s).

**Does not issue tokens.** After this call, the user must:
1. Log in via `POST /auth/login` → receives `{ requires_verification: true, channel }`.
2. Enter the OTP via `POST /auth/verify-login` → tokens issued.

Alternatively: validate token first via `GET /auth/invite/validate`.

token.description: Raw invite token from the invitation link.

200.description: Account created — OTP(s) sent, proceed to login

message.example: Account created. Please verify your account to log in.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:User`

Scoping rules:
- `platform-admin` sees all users and may filter by `org_id`.
- `org-admin` and `dispatcher` see only users within their own org (scoped automatically).

The `phone_number` field is masked to `+250***123` format for non-admin callers.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Platform admin only — filter by organization.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:User`

For `driver` and `passenger` roles the CASL rule carries condition `{ id: userId }`.
The route gate passes (the rule exists), but the service returns `403` for any ID
other than the caller's own.

patch.description:
**Required permission:** `update:User`

Field notes:
- `role_slugs` — full role replacement (all existing roles are deleted and replaced).
  Requires `assign_role:User` (`platform-admin` only).
- `status: suspended` — immediately blacklists all active access tokens in Redis
  (15 min TTL matching the JWT TTL) and sends a `security.account_suspended`
  notification to the target user via their `notif_channel` preference.
- `org_id` — reassigns the user to a different org (`platform-admin` only).

status.description: Suspended triggers Redis token blacklist and a push/SMS notification to the user.

role_slugs.description: Replaces all current roles atomically. Requires `assign_role:User` (`platform-admin` only). To add a role without removing others, fetch the user first and merge.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `delete:User` → `platform-admin` only.

Sets `deleted_at` (soft delete) and revokes all active refresh tokens.
Active access tokens are blacklisted in Redis for 15 min (matching the JWT TTL)
so in-flight sessions cannot be used after deletion.

204.description: User soft-deleted — tokens revoked and blacklisted

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:Role`

`platform-admin` sees all roles (platform-wide and org-specific).
`org-admin` sees only roles belonging to their org plus managed platform roles.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `create:Role`

Creates a new role with the supplied grant patterns. Patterns must be valid
entries from the permission catalog (`GET /permissions`) combined with a valid scope.

`org-admin` callers may only assign patterns with scope `own` or `org` — not `platform`.
The role is scoped to the caller's org automatically.

`platform-admin` callers may create platform-wide roles (omit `org_id`) or org-scoped
roles (include `org_id`).

Duplicate slug returns `409`.

org_id.description: `platform-admin` only — scope the role to a specific org. Omit for a platform-wide role.

patterns.description: Grant patterns in `subject:action:scope` format. Use `GET /permissions` for valid subjects and actions.

403.description: Forbidden or privilege escalation attempt (scope exceeds caller's max scope)

422.description: Invalid pattern format or unknown subject/action

error.message: "user:badaction:org" is not a valid grant pattern

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:Role`

`org-admin` may only access roles within their own org or managed platform roles.

patch.description:
**Required permission:** `update:Role`

Only the `name` field is mutable. To change grants, use `POST /roles/{id}/grants`
or `DELETE /roles/{id}/grants/{grantId}`.

Returns `403 MANAGED_ROLE_IMMUTABLE` if the role has `is_managed: true`.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `delete:Role`

Returns `403 MANAGED_ROLE_IMMUTABLE` for managed roles.
Users currently assigned this role will lose it on their next token refresh.

post.summary: Add a grant to a role
post.description:
**Required permission:** `update:Role`

Adds a single grant pattern to the role. Idempotent — if the pattern already exists,
the current role is returned unchanged (no error, no duplicate created).

Returns `403 MANAGED_ROLE_IMMUTABLE` for managed roles.

Grant changes take effect on the user's **next token refresh** — not immediately.

200.description: Role with the new grant added (or unchanged if already present)

403.description: Forbidden, managed role, or scope escalation

delete.summary: Remove a grant from a role
delete.description:
**Required permission:** `update:Role`

Returns `403 MANAGED_GRANT_IMMUTABLE` if the grant has `is_managed: true`.
Returns `403 MANAGED_ROLE_IMMUTABLE` if the role itself is managed.

Changes take effect on the user's **next token refresh**.

403.description: Forbidden, managed role, or managed grant

get.description:
**Required permission:** `read:Role`

Returns all valid `(subject, action)` pairs recognized by the platform, ordered by
`group → subject → action`. Use this to build the pattern picker UI when creating
or editing roles — never allow free-text pattern entry.

The `code` field is the canonical `subject:action` string. Combine it with a scope
suffix to form a grant pattern: `"{code}:{scope}"` → e.g. `"user:read:org"`.
